summary: FQ-13P4 closes the FQ-13P arc by formalizing the variant-vs-animation distinction the spec requires and locking item-icon stability. A new frame_semantics authority in data/visual_assets.json states that the shared <id>_NN.png convention is consumed two DISTINCT ways: VARIANT categories (blocks/enemies/players) pick exactly one entry deterministically and hold it (an alternate visual form), while ANIMATION categories (opening) play the entries in order as time frames (prologue.gd cel_frame_index = (tick*8/TICK_HZ) % n, an 8fps loop). Item icons are canonical-only and cached, so an inventory stack never shows a different icon between refreshes. scripts/asset_audit.py gains ANIMATION_CATEGORIES = {opening}; opening _NN files report as frames=N ANIMATION rather than a pick-one variant pool and are never flagged AVAILABLE_NOT_CONSUMED, while sequence-gap and dimension checks still apply. Two fq13p4_* smoke checks confirm item_icon returns the same texture across refreshes for both an art-backed item and a swatch-only item (with no item variant pool) and that the manifest documents both VARIANT and ANIMATION plus the opening category. README suite count updated 283 -> 298 and docs/UI_ASSET_GAPS.md records the arc complete (P0-P4). Block/item variation follow-through: the block variant mechanism stays live-but-unauthored (drop-in art, no code, no new block IDs), and item-icon stability is now smoke-locked. Suite 296 -> 298. No runtime behavior changed - the opening already played frames in order; this run documents and audit-enforces the distinction.
scope: FQ-13P4 per the FQ-13P arc - opening variant-vs-animation-frame distinction (frame_semantics + audit ANIMATION_CATEGORIES), item-icon stability lock, block/item variation follow-through, README suite-count refresh, and arc closure. No runtime behavior change, no new art authored.
next_action: FQ-14: goal panel, tutorial prompts, and playtest checklist. Authored art (sprites, block/player variant pools) continues via docs/ASSET_ROADMAP.md.
